note: Administer Users by Role delegates scoped user administration via static + dynamically generated permissions plus a role-classification settings form. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts (permission machine names, operations, role classification, the access service) an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want a 'sub-admin' user who can edit and cancel only some users, chosen by the target user's roles, without giving them full 'Administer users'. Which contrib module does this and what is the core idea?
evals.expected_output: Administer Users by Role: replaces core's all-or-nothing 'administer users' with per-operation, per-role permissions; a sub-admin may act on a user only if they have access to all of that user's roles.

evals.prompt: What are the four base per-operation permission machine names Administer Users by Role provides for acting on users whose roles are all 'allowed'?
evals.expected_output: edit users by role, cancel users by role, view users by role, role-assign users by role (operations edit, cancel, view, assign-roles against allowed roles).

evals.prompt: In Administer Users by Role, when a role is classified 'Custom', what per-role permission machine names get generated? Give the pattern and an example for a role id 'editor'.
evals.expected_output: Pattern '{op} users with role {rid}' for op in edit/cancel/view/role-assign, e.g. 'edit users with role editor' — only works combined with the matching base permission.

evals.prompt: How do I control which roles a sub-admin can manage in Administer Users by Role? Name the three classification options and where they are set.
evals.expected_output: On the settings form (route administerusersbyrole.settings, Admin > People) classify each role Allowed (safe), Forbidden (unsafe) or Custom (perm), stored in the administerusersbyrole.settings config 'roles' map.

evals.prompt: Which Administer Users by Role permission lets a sub-admin create new accounts, and which one grants access to the People list (showing only editable users)?
evals.expected_output: 'create users' grants creating new accounts; 'access users overview' grants the People/manage-users list, filtered to only users the sub-admin can edit.

evals.prompt: In Administer Users by Role, how can a sub-admin assign roles, and which permission governs it? Can they assign roles without being able to edit the user?
evals.expected_output: Via the user edit form role checkboxes or the core Add/Remove role bulk actions, governed by 'role-assign users by role' (+ per-role variants). Yes: allowed if they can edit the user OR can assign all roles the user already has, and can assign every changed role.

evals.prompt: Programmatically on a Drupal site, which Administer Users by Role service and method check whether an account may perform an operation on a user with given roles, and which method lists the roles an account may act on?
evals.expected_output: The administerusersbyrole.access service (AccessManager): access($roles, $operation, $account) returns an AccessResult (allowed only if every role passes); listRoles($operation, $account) returns the actionable role ids.

evals.prompt: Why should you NOT give a sub-admin the core 'Administer users' or 'Administer permissions' permission when using Administer Users by Role?
evals.expected_output: Holding core 'administer users' (or 'administer permissions') bypasses this module's checks entirely — the sub-admin would then be able to manage all users regardless of role classification.
